LootArmorVnums.brief: A collection of constants representing unique virtual numbers (VNUMs) for various armor pieces, accessories, and related items used in the game's loot and equipment systems.
LootArmorVnums.details: This group defines a set of constexpr integer constants that assign specific VNUMs to different armor components, body parts, and equipment items such as headgear, torso armor, limbs, shields, and special objects like floating or holding items. These constants provide a standardized and maintainable way to reference game objects, ensuring consistent spawning, identification, and management across the game's codebase. By organizing these identifiers within a dedicated group, the system supports reliable equipment handling, loot generation, and item interactions, facilitating clear and error-resistant game logic related to character gear and accessories.
LootArmorVnums.notes: The constants are organized to correspond to specific body parts and item types, promoting intuitive mapping between game objects and their identifiers. This organization helps prevent errors from using magic numbers and simplifies updates or additions to the armor VNUMs.
LootArmorVnums.rationale: The group exists to centralize and standardize the identification of armor and equipment objects within the game, enabling consistent handling in loot generation, equipment management, and game mechanics. By providing clear, maintainable constants, it supports system scalability and reduces the risk of mismatched or hardcoded values, contributing to a robust and organized code architecture.
LootArmorVnums.name: LootArmorVnums

OBJ_VNUM_LIGHT.brief: The constant `OBJ_VNUM_LIGHT` stores the virtual number (vnum) identifier for light source objects in the game.
OBJ_VNUM_LIGHT.details: This constant defines the vnum used to uniquely identify light source items within the game’s object database. Initialized with the value 24356, it ensures consistent referencing of this specific light object across various modules, such as spawning, inventory management, and interaction logic. By using this constant, the codebase maintains clarity and reduces errors related to hardcoded vnum values, enabling reliable creation and detection of light sources during gameplay scenarios.
OBJ_VNUM_LIGHT.notes: The constant is typically used in object creation functions and condition checks to handle light sources uniformly. Its fixed value aligns with the game's object database, facilitating easy updates if the vnum changes in the future.
OBJ_VNUM_LIGHT.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to reference the specific light source object, supporting game mechanics that involve illumination, such as lighting rooms or characters' equipment, while avoiding magic numbers scattered throughout the code.
OBJ_VNUM_LIGHT.name: OBJ_VNUM_LIGHT

OBJ_VNUM_FINGER.notes: Using a dedicated constant for the finger armor vnum helps prevent errors from hardcoded values and facilitates easy updates if the vnum changes in the future.
OBJ_VNUM_FINGER.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable reference to the finger armor object, supporting consistent handling and manipulation of this item throughout the game's codebase.
OBJ_VNUM_FINGER.name: OBJ_VNUM_FINGER

OBJ_VNUM_NECK.name: OBJ_VNUM_NECK

OBJ_VNUM_TORSO.brief: The constant representing the virtual number for torso armor objects in the game.
OBJ_VNUM_TORSO.details: This constant defines the unique identifier (VNUM) used to reference torso armor items within the game's codebase. The constant is utilized in various parts of the code, such as spawning, equipping, and inventory management, to ensure consistent and clear references to torso armor objects. By using this named constant, the code becomes more readable and less prone to errors associated with hardcoded numeric values.
OBJ_VNUM_TORSO.notes: The constant is stored as a compile-time constant, ensuring efficient usage and preventing modification. It is part of the LootArmorVnums namespace, grouping related armor object identifiers together for better organization.
OBJ_VNUM_TORSO.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to reference the torso armor object prototype, facilitating consistent handling of such items across the game's systems and reducing the likelihood of errors from using magic numbers.
OBJ_VNUM_TORSO.name: OBJ_VNUM_TORSO
